g.data-narration: Welcome to Analysis of Covariance. This powerful technique combines ANOVA and regression for precise group comparisons.
g.text: Combining ANOVA and Regression for Precise Analysis

g.data-narration: ANCOVA combines ANOVA and linear regression to compare group means while controlling for continuous covariate effects.
g.text: • Compares group means after adjusting for

g.data-narration: ANCOVA increases precision by removing covariate-related variability from error, improving statistical power.
The ANCOVA model equation shows how dependent variable relates to treatment, covariate, and error components.
ANCOVA requires six key assumptions: linearity, homogeneity of regression slopes, independence, normality, and homoscedasticity.
Homogeneity of regression slopes assumes parallel relationships between covariate and outcome across all groups.
One-way ANCOVA tests group differences on one dependent variable while controlling for a single covariate.
Two-way ANCOVA examines main effects of two factors and their interaction while controlling for covariates.
Adjusted means represent group means if all groups had equal covariate values, controlling for covariate differences.
g.text: Ȳi(adj) = Adjusted mean for group i

g.data-narration: This example compares memory usage of three hash table implementations while controlling for key type (string vs integer).
g.text: Example 1: One-Way ANCOVA - Hash Table Comparison
Compare memory usage of 3 hash table implementations (Linear Probing, Chaining, Cuckoo)

g.data-narration: This example examines bug detection rates across testing methodologies and development environments while controlling for code complexity.
g.text: Example 2: Two-Way ANCOVA - Bug Detection Study

g.data-narration: ANCOVA applies to machine learning comparisons, controlling for training/test ratios when comparing algorithm accuracy.
ANCOVA combines ANOVA and regression to compare adjusted group means, increasing precision while controlling for covariates.
g.text: • ANCOVA = ANOVA on residuals after removing covariate effect
• Six assumptions must be checked before analysis
• Adjusted means represent group means at equal covariate values
• Increases precision and statistical power by reducing error variance

g.data-narration: Remember: Know the ANCOVA model equation, all six assumptions, adjusted means formula, and how to test homogeneity of regression slopes.
